SemiAnalysis.text:
**New this period:**
- Nvidia has introduced a GPU debt backstop program to support Neoclouds and broaden compute availability beyond hyperscalers and large AI labs.
- The program aims to evolve the GPU financing market, support Neocloud growth, and provide lenders with a credible backstop to lend to Neoclouds with varied client books and rental contract terms.
- Nvidia's backstop program has a six-year term, with pre-agreed price levels varying over time, and Nvidia shares in a portion of the Neocloud's revenue earned above the backstop level.
- The program enables Neoclouds to assemble the AI Project Trinity: Capital, Offtake, and Datacenter, making it easier for them to secure funding, offtake contracts, and datacenter capacity.
- Nvidia is also backstopping datacenter leases to further support Neocloud growth and reshape the GPU market structure.

**Details:**
**Nvidia GPU Debt Backstop Unleashes the AI Project Trinity: Capital, Offtake and Datacenters** (https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/nvidia-gpu-debt-backstop-unleashes)
- The core thesis is that Nvidia's GPU debt backstop program will unlock the growth of the AI compute market by providing a credible backstop for Neoclouds and supporting the evolution of the GPU financing market.
- Named companies and their roles: Nvidia (provider of GPU debt backstop program), Google (hyperscaler), Amazon (hyperscaler), Meta (hyperscaler), Microsoft (hyperscaler), Oracle (hyperscaler).
- Quantitative figures: $7.1T (projected AI debt financing market size by 2029), $2T (annual AI Capex in 2028), $11.1T (cumulative AI Capex from 2024 to 2029), $2.36 (average backstop price level over six-year period), 5 years (typical offtake period), 6 years (term of Nvidia's backstop program).
- Supply chain and value chain relationships: Nvidia provides GPUs to Neoclouds, which rent them to customers (including hyperscalers and AI labs); datacenter operators provide colocation services to Neoclouds.
- Technology transitions: The AI compute market is shifting from cashflow funding to debt financing, with Nvidia's backstop program supporting this transition.
- Competitive positioning: Nvidia's backstop program is expected to broaden the base of buyers beyond just a few hyperscalers, which will benefit Neoclouds and other market participants.
- Capital allocation signals: Nvidia's backstop program is a significant investment in the AI compute market, and the company expects to gain substantial revenue from it.
- Regulatory or policy signals: None explicitly mentioned in the article.

Andrej Karpathy \u2013 Blog.text:
**New this period:**
- Andrej Karpathy releases a new art project called microgpt, a single file of 200 lines of pure Python that trains and infers a GPT model.
- The microgpt project simplifies large language models to their bare essentials and is available on GitHub and as a Google Colab notebook.
- The project uses a dataset of 32,000 names and a simple tokenizer that assigns one integer to each unique character in the dataset.

**Details:**
**[microgpt](http://karpathy.github.io/2026/02/12/microgpt/)**
- The core thesis of this piece is the release of microgpt, a simplified large language model implemented in a single Python file.
- Companies mentioned: GitHub, Google (for Google Colab notebook).
- The specific role of GitHub is to host the microgpt source code, and the specific role of Google is to provide the platform for the Google Colab notebook.
- Quantitative figures: 200 lines of code, 32,000 names in the dataset, 26 possible lowercase characters (a-z) in the tokenizer, and 1 special token (BOS) for a total vocabulary size of 27.
- Supply chain or value chain relationships: the project relies on the GitHub platform for hosting the code and the Google Colab notebook for interactive execution.
- Technology transitions: microgpt simplifies large language models by reducing them to their essential components, making it easier to understand and implement these models.
- The project uses a simple tokenizer and autograd engine, which are key components of large language models, and demonstrates their usage in a simplified setting.
- The autograd engine is implemented using a Value class that wraps scalar numbers and tracks how they were computed, allowing for automatic differentiation and gradient computation.
- No specific capital allocation signals or regulatory signals are mentioned in this piece.

Ethan Mollick \u2013 One Useful Thing.text:
**New this period:**
- AI models are improving at an accelerating rate, with evidence showing capability gains in areas such as software development and complex problem-solving.
- The way people use AI is changing, with a shift from co-intelligence to using long-running, smart, and self-correcting AI systems, or agents.
- Agents are being adopted across various functions, including non-tech roles, and are changing the nature of work, with users increasingly acting as managers of AI systems.

**Details:**
**The twilight of the chatbots (https://www.oneusefulthing.org/p/the-twilight-of-the-chatbots)**
- The article discusses the rapid improvement of AI models, with leading American AI labs such as Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google releasing more powerful models at an accelerating rate.
- The author mentions that government interventions have temporarily stopped access to two of the most powerful models, Claude Fable and GPT-5.6.
- Quantitative figures include:
  - Opus 4.7 was able to build a software package in 14 hours that would take 2-17 weeks of human engineering work, at a cost of $251 in tokens.
  - Fable was able to work autonomously for 9 hours to execute complex software projects that would have taken a team over a week to do.
- Supply chain and value chain relationships include:
  - American companies Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google are leading the development of frontier AI models.
  - Chinese companies are developing near-frontier AI models, which are open weights models that can be used or modified by anyone after release.
- Technology transitions include:
  - A shift from using AI as a co-intelligence to using long-running, smart, and self-correcting AI systems, or agents.
  - The development of harnesses and apps, such as Claude Code or OpenAI's Codex, that give AI access to tools and an environment to act in.
- Competitive positioning:
  - American companies are currently leading the development of frontier AI models, but Chinese companies are catching up with near-frontier models.
  - The use of agents is changing the nature of work, with users increasingly acting as managers of AI systems, and experts in various domains are able to use agents effectively.
- Capital allocation signals:
  - The author mentions a joint study by OpenAI and academic economists, which shows the rapid adoption of agents within OpenAI.
  - A separate study of Claude Code users found that software engineers had a similar success rate to other professions when using Claude Code on coding tasks.
- Regulatory or policy signals:
  - Government interventions have temporarily stopped access to two of the most powerful AI models, Claude Fable and GPT-5.6.
  - The author mentions that AI is not capable of being a real cybersecurity threat until suddenly it is, causing sudden and improvised policy changes at the highest level of government.

Arxiv \u2013 Trading & Market Microstruc.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers detected 1,012 persistent wallet cohorts on the Solana pump.fun marketplace, with the top cohort appearing among the first 10 buyers of 42 launches.
- A survival analysis of 832,941 Solana pump.fun token launches found a pooled graduation rate of 0.198%, with social-channel presence exerting a large effect on graduation.
- A precision audit of a production filter stack in Solana DEX trading yielded a conservative save-to-miss ratio of 3.7 : 1.
- A study proposed a Gabor--Epps uncertainty principle for practical trading, suggesting six simple rules of thumb for traders operating at market-making frequencies.
- Researchers studied a one-period limit order market with informed traders, noise traders, and competitive liquidity suppliers, characterizing equilibrium by a fixed-point integral equation.
- A minimal limit-order-book model reproduced the square-root law of market impact, with order splitting and liquidity replenishment jointly identified as necessary mechanisms.
- A study derived an operational-time variance kernel for a latent-order-book reaction boundary, separating structural boundary cumulant, clock projection, and pricing-measure choice.

**Details:**
**Coordinated Sniper Cohorts on Pump.fun (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.02795)**
- The study analyzed 1,578,333 buyer observations from 166,098 token launches on the Solana pump.fun marketplace.
- 1,012 persistent wallet cohorts were detected, with 153 qualifying as high-tier and 22 hitting 20 launches.
- The top cohort appeared among the first 10 buyers of 42 distinct launches over 11 days at average first-buyer rank 2.29.
- Cohort-touched launches showed a +132.3% lift in first-30-minute buyer count and +136.5% lift in SOL inflow.
- The study released the full cohort catalogue, detection code, and robustness artefacts as RED-COHORT-2026-v1 under CC-BY-4.0.

**Pump.fun Graduation Regime Windows (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.02823)**
- The study presented a Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional-hazards survival analysis of 832,941 Solana pump.fun token launches.
- The pooled graduation rate was 0.198% (Wilson 95% CI [0.189%, 0.208%]), a 3.18x decline from the 0.63% rate reported by Marino et al. (2026).
- Social-channel presence exerted a large effect on graduation, with launches advertising Telegram graduating at 1.485% versus 0.166% without.
- The study released the 860,213-launch dataset under CC-BY-4.0 on Zenodo at concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20633486.

**Outcome-Classified Precision Auditing of Filter Rules (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.02830)**
- The study reported a precision audit of a production filter stack against a 13-day window of post-rejection forward-market observations on Solana DEX trading.
- The audit yielded 99,510 follow-up samples across 2,402 unique rejection events spanning eight active filter rules.
- The headline result was a conservative save-to-miss ratio of 3.7 : 1 from windowed measured-drawdown saves alone.
- The study tested the interpretive premise of a wider tier against the deposited lifecycle data of a separately-published benchmark (RED-2400).

**Exact conditional simulation of Point processes (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.03239)**
- The study addressed the conditional simulation of point processes under perturbed intensities.
- Given an observed counting process, the study characterized the conditional law of the latent Poisson random measure in a thinning representation.
- The study yielded an exact, event-driven algorithm that reconstructs counterfactual paths on a common randomness source.

**A Gabor--Epps uncertainty principle for traders (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.04130)**
- The study proposed a Gabor--Epps uncertainty principle for practical trading.
- The principle suggests six simple rules of thumb for traders operating at market-making frequencies.
- The study argued that high-frequency correlation is not observed in clock time alone, but is resolved through market activity, order-flow overlap, and finite coupling response.

**A Limit Order Market with Uncertain Informed Trading Participation (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.04221)**
- The study characterized equilibrium by a fixed-point integral equation for the marginal cost function.
- The study established existence of equilibrium for bounded asset values.
- The study analyzed large order asymptotics, finding that equilibrium price impact follows a power law whose exponent is determined jointly by the asset value tail and the full distribution of the informed trader count.

**Order Splitting and Liquidity Replenishment Are Jointly Necessary for the Square-Root Law of Market Impact (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.04280)**
- The study used a minimal limit-order-book model populated by heterogeneous interacting agents.
- The study calibrated the model against the Tokyo Stock Exchange benchmark (〈δ〉 = 0.489).
- The study found that order splitting and liquidity replenishment are jointly necessary for the square-root law of market impact.
- The study performed counterfactual ablation by selectively suppressing each component, finding that removing order splitting or liquidity replenishment collapses δ.

**Reaction-boundary variance and adjoint-consistent local-volatility projection (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.05011)**
- The study derived an operational-time variance kernel for a latent-order-book reaction boundary.
- The study separated structural boundary cumulant, clock projection, and pricing-measure choice.
- The study argued that adjoin consistency is a reality constraint on operational-to-calendar time projection, disciplining non-unique time and identifying candidate calendar-time pricing systems.

Arxiv \u2013 Statistical Finance (q-fin..text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers compare the performance of pretrained time series foundation models (TSFMs) with econometric benchmarks for forecasting realized volatility.
- A study examines the limitations of local Gaussian correlation (LGC) estimates in measuring dependence in the joint tails of heavy-tailed data.
- A new diagnostic method is proposed to evaluate factor models using a characteristic-axis integral approach.
- An extension of the cap-axis integral diagnostic is presented to measure factor-model pricing errors as bridge-alpha curves.

**Details:**
**Forecasting Realized Volatility with Time Series Foundation Models: A Comparison with Econometric Benchmarks** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.05291)
- The study compares the performance of nine zero-shot TSFMs with eight econometric specifications, including the Heterogeneous Autoregressive (HAR) family.
- The VOLARE dataset is used, covering 50 assets in equities, foreign exchange, and futures, and three forecast horizons.
- Only one small model, Tiny Time Mixers (TTM), beats the Log-HAR benchmark at every horizon, and by a narrow margin.
- The other foundation models do not improve on Log-HAR, and the econometric benchmarks remain competitive throughout.
- A Mincer--Zarnowitz recalibration shows that much of the short-horizon advantage reflects better-scaled forecasts rather than better prediction of volatility dynamics.

**Local Gaussian Correlation in the Tails: A Scarcity Diagnostic, an Optimal Local Bandwidth, and the Limits of Adaptivity** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.03888)
- The study examines the limitations of local Gaussian correlation (LGC) estimates in measuring dependence in the joint tails of heavy-tailed data.
- A diagnostic is proposed to identify the scarcity of data in the tails, and a location-specific AMISE-optimal bandwidth for LGC is derived.
- The study maps the regime in which adaptivity does help and shows that the binding constraint on tail LGC is data scarcity, not bandwidth placement.

**Any Axes Are Allowed: A Characteristic-Axis Integral Diagnosis of Factor Models** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.05091)
- The study extends the cap-axis integral diagnostic to general characteristic axes and measures factor-model pricing errors as bridge-alpha curves.
- The method is applied to 1967-2024 CRSP data, and value, profitability, investment, and momentum axes show systematic sign reversals.
- HML and CMA overcorrect significantly, whereas RMW and UMD largely flatten their axes.

**A Cap-Axis Integral Diagnostic of Factor Models** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.01765)
- The study proposes a cap-axis integral diagnostic for factor-model evaluation.
- The diagnostic studies one subspace by lifting pricing errors into a bridge-alpha curve along the market-capitalization rank axis.
- The method is applied to 1967-2024 CRSP data, and the cap-axis norm is distinct from Sharpe gain and size exposure.

Arxiv \u2013 Portfolio Management (q-fin.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers examine portfolio optimization and tail-risk analytics for actively managed investment funds using daily Bloomberg data from 4 December 2020 to 24 December 2025.
- A preference-fitting framework is proposed to elicit utility functions and develop a PHARA approximation method for portfolio selection.
- Causal separation is formalized as a structural condition for portfolio problems, leading to a closed-form projected Markowitz solution.
- A spectral generalization of the variance ratio is proposed to decompose long-horizon equity-return dynamics into separate memory components.
- Look-ahead-freedom is formalized as temporal non-interference, and a pipeline calculus is developed to separate datum availability from reference time.
- Asset pre-selection is explored for a cardinality-constrained index tracking portfolio with optional enhancement using S&P 500 data.
- Adaptive partitioning and learning are introduced for stochastic control of diffusion processes, enabling efficient learning in unbounded domains.

**Details:**
**Portfolio Optimization and Tail-Risk Analytics of Actively Managed ETFs** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.03082)
- The study evaluates buy-and-hold, mean--variance, CVaR-based, and tangency-type strategies under long-only and long--short constraints.
- The sample consists predominantly of actively managed ETFs, with PTTRX as an actively managed fixed-income mutual-fund comparator.
- Quantitative figures: 30 funds, 4 December 2020 to 24 December 2025, daily Bloomberg data.
- Tangency-type portfolios are generally the strongest competitors to the buy-and-hold benchmark in cumulative and risk-adjusted terms.

**Preference-fitting Framework: Elicited Utility Function and PHARA Approximation** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.04346)
- The utility function plays a core role in portfolio selection, but its specific form is typically hard to elicit.
- A preference-fitting method is proposed to obtain the elicited utility function through intuitive probability-wealth pairs.
- A piecewise hyperbolic absolute risk aversion (PHARA) utility approximation method is developed.

**Causal Separation, Conditional Risk, and Projected Markowitz Portfolios** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.05320)
- Causal separation is formalized as a structural condition on a portfolio problem, leading to a closed-form projected Markowitz solution.
- The condition derives a complete static portfolio theory, including a diagonal-plus-low-rank conditional covariance.
- A conditional efficient-frontier theorem and a Hansen-Jagannathan bound are proven.

**A Spectral Generalisation of the Variance Ratio** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.03858)
- A multivariate generalisation of the Lo-MacKinlay variance ratio is proposed to decompose long-horizon equity-return dynamics.
- The framework identifies a five-factor model capturing persistent, antipersistent, and multi-scale memory in returns and volatility.
- Quantitative figures: four U.S. portfolio panels, a European replication, and seven stylised facts of long-horizon equity dynamics.

**Look-Ahead-Freedom as Temporal Non-Interference** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.04958)
- Look-ahead-freedom is formalized as temporal non-interference over a time-indexed information lattice.
- A pipeline calculus is developed to separate datum availability from reference time, and a type-and-effect system is proposed.

**Asset pre-selection for a cardinality constrained index tracking portfolio** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18609)
- Eight pre-selection procedures are compared: forward selection or backward elimination, using ordinary least squares or least absolute deviation regression.
- Quantitative figures: $900 billion, S&P 500 data, out-of-sample tracking errors decrease according to the inverse of the square root of cardinality.
- Cardinalities of the order 10 to 20 are most effective for enhanced returns.

**Adaptive Partitioning and Learning for Stochastic Control of Diffusion Processes** (https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.14991)
- A model-based algorithm is introduced that adaptively partitions the joint state-action space for controlled diffusion processes.
- The algorithm maintains estimators of drift, volatility, and rewards within each partition, refining the discretization whenever estimation bias exceeds statistical confidence.
- Quantitative figures: polynomially growing rewards, regret bounds depend on the problem horizon, state dimension, reward growth order, and a newly defined notion of zooming dimension.

FinAnEx \u2013 SUFE Finance Research For.text:
**New this period:**
- Researchers discover that "prediction bias" is a unified mechanism explaining individual investor stock trading decisions, including buying, selling, and funding allocation.
- A study of 959 Danish investors finds that those with higher prediction bias tend to buy stocks with higher past returns and sell stocks with lower capital gains.
- The study's findings suggest that prediction bias affects market timing behavior, with investors with higher bias increasing funding allocations after strong market performance.

**Details:**
**Extrapolators And Contrarians: Forecast Bias And Individual Investor Stock Trading** (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2026.104291)
- The research question is how prediction bias affects individual investor stock trading decisions.
- The method involves a controlled laboratory experiment with 959 Danish investors, where they predict a randomly generated time series and receive rewards based on accuracy.
- The key finding is that prediction bias, measured as a core parameter, significantly influences buying, selling, and funding allocation decisions.
- Named companies and their roles are not mentioned in the study.
- Quantitative figures include:
  - 959: number of Danish investors participating in the experiment
  - 11 years: duration of the administrative trading records analyzed
  - 3.0 percentage points: average increase in excess return for stocks purchased by investors with higher prediction bias
  - 2011-2021: time period of the trading records analyzed
- Supply chain and value chain relationships are not explicitly described in the study.
- Technology transitions are not mentioned in the study.
- The study's findings suggest that investors with higher prediction bias tend to buy stocks with higher past returns and sell stocks with lower capital gains, while those with lower bias tend to do the opposite.
- The study's results have implications for understanding individual investor behavior and market timing.

The Batch \u2013 Andrew Ng (DeepLearning.text:
**New this period:**
- OpenAI launched a preview of its GPT-5.6 family, including a top-tier model comparable to Anthropic's Claude 5 Mythos, but with limited access to U.S. government-approved organizations.
- OpenAI's GPT-5.6 models achieved strong performance in coding, cybersecurity, and biology, with GPT-5.6 Sol setting the state of the art on Terminal-Bench 2.1.
- The U.S. government has begun to control the launch of top-performing AI models, with OpenAI limiting the launch of GPT-5.6 to around 20 government-approved organizations.

**Details:**
**The Batch – Andrew Ng (DeepLearning.AI)**(https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/issue-360)
- OpenAI announced a preview of its GPT-5.6 family, including GPT-5.6 Sol, GPT-5.6 Terra, and GPT-5.6 Luna, with limited access to U.S. government-approved organizations.
- Companies mentioned: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Cerebras, DeepLearning.AI.
- Roles: OpenAI (developer of GPT-5.6 models), Anthropic (developer of Claude Mythos 5 model), Cerebras (will offer access to GPT-5.6 Sol at speeds up to 750 tokens per second).
- Quantitative figures: 
  - GPT-5.6 Sol price: $5/$0.50/$30 per 1 million input/cached/output tokens.
  - GPT-5.6 Terra price: $2.50/$0.25/$15 per 1 million input/cached/output tokens.
  - GPT-5.6 Luna price: $1/$0.10/$6 per 1 million input/cached/output tokens.
  - GPT-5.6 Sol performance: 91.9% on Terminal-Bench 2.1, 88.8% on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (unspecified reasoning level), 73.5% on ExploitBench, 96.7% on OpenAI's internal capture-the-flag security challenges.
  - SecureBio test results: GPT-5.6 Sol scored 68.3% on World-Class Bio, a nearly 10-point improvement over GPT-5.5 (59.7%).
- Supply chain and value chain relationships: OpenAI partnered with the U.S. government to limit the launch of GPT-5.6 to approved organizations.
- Technology transitions: The GPT-5.6 family brings concerns about AI's security implications to the lower-priced tier, with cheaper and faster models drawing more scrutiny.
- Competitive positioning: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol outperformed Anthropic's Claude Mythos 5 on Terminal-Bench 2.1, but was narrowly outperformed by Claude Mythos Preview on ExploitBench.
